title: ruscus gayae
intro: Ruscus gayae, also known as the Ruscus aculeatus, is a type of flowering plant in the family Colchicaceae. It is a perennial plant native to Europe and parts of Asia, known for its medicinal properties derived from its roots, leaves, and berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, Ruscus gayae is often used as a diuretic and to treat conditions such as kidney stones and urinary tract infections, typically in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures.
